feedback.details: Diagram passes mmdc validation but violates contract: contains 8 container nodes instead of exactly 7. backend/c2-container.md:16-31 defines: llm-switch, consul-agent, vault-agent (should be vault-server per contract), nomad-client, qwen-local, nemotron-local, frontier-api-gateway, AND ai-app (line 31 - extra). The 'ai-app' container has no incoming 'uses' relationship from other containers (only outgoing to llm-switch), creating an orphan node condition. Contract requires exactly 7 containers with vault-server, not vault-agent.

Diagram contains all required components but misnames vault-server as vault-agent (line 24). While architecturally correct for Nomad, this deviates from contract specification. All C4 IDs present and 'uses' relationships defined via Rel() syntax (lines 34-40). Missing explicit 'contains' relationships as specified in contract (line 13-43 section). The container ai-app at line 31 is an External System but lacks proper External boundary marker.

HCL passes structural requirements: GPU syntax correct at line 109 (`gpu = 1`), health check at lines 77-83 with correct /health/ready path, 10s interval, 3s timeout. Vault config at lines 118-132 includes token_renewal=true. Minor issue: policies 'llm-switch-read' and 'llm-switch-write' at line 119 are defined but contract requires explicit ACL roles showing service account binding and path restrictions.

Excellent coverage. OpenAPI 3.0 spec at lines 169-684 includes all authentication (X-API-Key header at line 188, OAuth2 at lines 189-196), rate limiting headers (lines 356-366), curl examples for all endpoints (lines 689-850), and HTTP status codes (table at lines 853-863). Missing only explicit error response JSON examples for 403 and 503 in curl example outputs - only success responses shown.

Cites incorrect line numbers in technology-choices.md. backend/c2-container.md:869,873,877 all reference 'lines 1-2' but lines 1-2 contain only document header ('# Technology Choices...'). Actual technology choices (golang, bifrost) are at lines 4-6 of technology-choices.md. Go version 1.21+ (line 869), Docker base image gcr.io/distroless/static-debian11 (line 877), bifrost v0.4.0+ (line 873) are correct. Missing required performance benchmark references or security audit results as specified in contract.

Document follows structural standards: YAML frontmatter at lines 1-5 with author/date/version, heading hierarchy maintained (H1 at line 7, H2 at lines 9,13,57,165,865,913,925,952,983). Code blocks specify language identifiers (mermaid:15, hcl:59, yaml:169, bash:689). Cannot verify markdownlint compliance without execution. Minor: line 120 'change_mode' is Nomad-specific but lacks context or documentation reference.

Complete error handling documentation at lines 925-951. Timeout values correct (30s LLM at line 928, 5s Consul at line 929, 10s Vault at line 930). Retry logic documented (lines 932-934: 3 attempts, 1s/2s/4s exponential backoff). Circuit breaker at lines 936-939 (5 failures/30s, 60s open state). DLQ with Redis sidecar at lines 139-158 and PagerDuty integration at line 944 with threshold (>10 entries/5min at line 945).

TLS 1.3 and cipher suites documented (lines 955-956). mTLS with 24h rotation (line 957). 90-day API key rotation (line 960). CRITICAL: Vault secrets path at line 962 uses '/secret/llm-switch/{environment}/{service}' but contract requires '/secret/c2/*' path structure. ACL policies defined at lines 963-966 but do not explicitly demonstrate the /secret/c2/* path restriction required by the sprint contract.

p99 latency SLA documented (line 986: <200ms under 1000 QPS). CRITICAL: Memory specification mismatch - contract requires '2GB container with OOMKilled prevention' but line 108 specifies 8192MB (8GB) container with 2GB GOMEMLIMIT at line 115. This provides 8GB container with 2GB heap limit, NOT 2GB container as required. CPU 4000 millicores correct (line 107). Connection limits 100 per instance (line 997). Load shedding at 80% CPU (line 999).

overall_summary: Round 8 evaluation reveals persistent structural deviations from Sprint 4 contract despite Round 7 passing at 9.8/10. The document is comprehensive but fails on specific quantitative requirements: (1) Mermaid diagram contains 8 containers instead of exactly 7 with vault-server renamed to vault-agent and extra ai-app container added; (2) Vault secrets path uses /secret/llm-switch/* instead of required /secret/c2/* path structure; (3) Container memory is 8GB not 2GB as specified; (4) Technology choices cite wrong line numbers (lines 1-2 vs actual lines 4-6 in technology-choices.md). These are not implementation errors but contract compliance failures. The API documentation and error handling sections remain excellent (9.0+ scores), but the security path mismatch, resource specification error, and diagram composition error prevent passing the sprint criteria.
